mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, boosting aggregate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects supply-side bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where INDPRO falls below its 12-month trend during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI exceeds 195.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate divergence condition yields a positive 20-day return in more than 50% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumer expenditure capacity, which directly lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns drops below 0.10, or predictive accuracy falls to chance levels.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while retail sales accelerate indicates inventory drawdowns and unsustainable consumption borrowing, leading to earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside stable inflation signals healthy capacity utilization without price pressures, supporting risk-on sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week during disinflationary periods.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL remains flat during disinflationary regimes.
